title: Add Azure Sentinel (Log Analytics) export target + Private deployment config examples
preview:
Context
We’re standardizing audit event schema v1 across Hosted/Dedicated/Private and expanding supported SIEM export targets. This PR adds an Azure Sentinel / Log Analytics sink to audit-log-exporter using the Azure Monitor Logs Ingestion API (DCE + DCR) and includes configuration examples for Private deployments.
...
text:
Context
We’re standardizing audit event schema v1 across Hosted/Dedicated/Private and expanding supported SIEM export targets. This PR adds an Azure Sentinel / Log Analytics sink to audit-log-exporter using the Azure Monitor Logs Ingestion API (DCE + DCR) and includes configuration examples for Private deployments.

Why
- Multiple enterprise/security reviews (and SUP-18477) requested first-class Sentinel support.
- Private customers want a supported path that doesn’t rely on “roll your own” forwarders.
- We need consistent payload validation + schema_version tagging before exporting to third-party systems (ties to ENG-4827).

What’s in this PR
1) New sink: azure_sentinel
- Adds a new output target: type=azure_sentinel
- Uses Azure AD OAuth2 client credentials to obtain an access token.
- Posts batches to Azure Monitor Logs Ingestion endpoint (DCE) for a configured Data Collection Rule...

title: Add Splunk HEC export target (batching, retry/backoff, delivery metrics)
preview:
## Summary
Adds a first-class Splunk HEC export target to audit-log-exporter. The target supports (1) bounded batching to control request volume and Splunk HEC payload size, (2) retry w/ exponential backoff + jitter for transient failures, and (3) explicit delivery visibility via metrics and structured logs.

This i...
text:
## Summary
Adds a first-class Splunk HEC export target to audit-log-exporter. The target supports (1) bounded batching to control request volume and Splunk HEC payload size, (2) retry w/ exponential backoff + jitter for transient failures, and (3) explicit delivery visibility via metrics and structured logs.

This is part of the audit logging standardization effort to ensure customers can reliably export normalized audit events into common SIEMs. Splunk HEC is a frequent enterprise requirement (see SUP-18477) and has been a blocker for at least two Private-deployment evaluations.

## Motivation / context
- Customers want to ingest audit logs into Splunk with predictable field semantics and stable delivery.
- Our current exporter supports HTTP generic sinks, but Splunk HEC has specific behavior and recommended payload structure (event wrapping, source/sourcetype, token auth, and status...

title: Add schema_version tagging, JSON Schema validation, and legacy event normalization to v1
preview:
## Summary
This PR updates the audit-log-exporter to:
- Tag all outgoing events with `schema_version` (default: `v1.0`).
- Validate outgoing payloads against the canonical Audit Log Schema v1 (JSON Schema) and emit exporter-side metrics when validation fails.
- Normalize legacy/heterogeneous producer payloads into t...
text:
## Summary
This PR updates the audit-log-exporter to:
- Tag all outgoing events with `schema_version` (default: `v1.0`).
- Validate outgoing payloads against the canonical Audit Log Schema v1 (JSON Schema) and emit exporter-side metrics when validation fails.
- Normalize legacy/heterogeneous producer payloads into the v1 canonical shape to eliminate field drift across event types.

This is the first step to making SIEM exports consistent across Hosted/Dedicated/Private deployments and to support SOC2/ISO evidence expectations (stable schema + documented backcompat behavior).

## Motivation
We have multiple event producers emitting “almost the same” semantics but with different field names and shapes. Customers exporting to Splunk/Sentinel/Datadog see inconsistent keys across event types (SUP-18477). Additionally, exporter retries can cause duplicates (SUP-18510); while idempotency is ...

title: Add schema + SIEM mappings for offline bundle verification audit events
preview: Context: Redwood Private offline bundle signing/verifying is landing for air-gapped installs. The installer now emits local audit events for bundle verification outcomes (success + specific failure reasons). Customers who forward audit logs to a SIEM via audit-log-exporter need these events to be recognized, normali...
text:
Context: Redwood Private offline bundle signing/verifying is landing for air-gapped installs. The installer now emits local audit events for bundle verification outcomes (success + specific failure reasons). Customers who forward audit logs to a SIEM via audit-log-exporter need these events to be recognized, normalized, and exported in a stable schema.

What this PR does:
- Adds new event definitions to audit-log-exporter’s schema catalog for bundle verification outcomes.
- Adds normalization/mapping rules for JSON (native), CEF, and LEEF output modes.
- Ensures failure reasons are exported as a bounded enum + optional details field (redaction-aware).
- Updates unit tests + golden fixtures for all output formats.

New events (normalized):
- redwood.private.bundle_verification.succeeded
- redwood.private.bundle_verification.failed

Normalized fields:
- event.name (string)
- event.outco...

title: Include KMS rotation audit events in export schema + parsers
preview: Context: Redwood is adding explicit audit log events for KMS rotation and cutover toggles (see redwood PR #18541). Several regulated customers ingest Redwood audit logs into their SIEM (Splunk/Elastic/Sentinel) via this exporter. Without schema updates, these events get emitted as `unknown_event_type` and are either...
text:
Context: Redwood is adding explicit audit log events for KMS rotation and cutover toggles (see redwood PR #18541). Several regulated customers ingest Redwood audit logs into their SIEM (Splunk/Elastic/Sentinel) via this exporter. Without schema updates, these events get emitted as `unknown_event_type` and are either dropped or lose critical fields needed for rotation evidence.

This PR updates the export schema and parsing/mapping code so KMS rotation events are exported as first-class, typed records with stable fields suitable for correlation and evidence collection.

What changed (high level):
- Added new event types to the exporter schema:
 - `kms.key_version.updated`
 - `kms.rotation.cutover.enabled`
 - `kms.rotation.cutover.disabled`
 - `kms.rotation.dual_read.enabled`
 - `kms.rotation.dual_read.disabled`
- Extended the parser to recognize these events from Redwood audit log enve...

title: Add e2e integration tests with Splunk HEC + Datadog Logs sandboxes
preview: Context: As part of SIEM-ready audit export beta, we need repeatable end-to-end confidence that the exporter can deliver to downstreams with realistic failure modes (auth errors, 429 throttling, payload validation issues) and that our retry/backoff + checkpointing semantics behave as expected. This PR adds an integr...
text: Context: As part of SIEM-ready audit export beta, we need repeatable end-to-end confidence that the exporter can deliver to downstreams with realistic failure modes (auth errors, 429 throttling, payload validation issues) and that our retry/backoff + checkpointing semantics behave as expected. This PR adds an integration test suite + docker-compose-ish harness that boots the exporter alongside (a) a Splunk-like HEC stub service and (b) a Datadog Logs intake stub service. It does not attempt to fully emulate Splunk/Datadog; it’s focused on request/response behavior the adapters depend on. Scope: - New `e2e/` harness with compose config for local + CI runs - Splunk HEC stub: validates token header, supports `/services/collector/event` + `/services/collector/health`, can simulate 429s and partial failures - Datadog stub: validates API key header, supports `/api/v2/logs`, can simulate 403...

title: S3 adapter: add SSE-KMS support + cross-account assume-role credentials (docs + IAM snippets)
preview: Summary: This extends the S3 destination adapter to support SSE-KMS (SSE with customer-managed keys) and an assume-role credential flow for cross-account bucket delivery. It also adds operator-facing documentation with minimal IAM policy snippets (bucket policy, role trust policy, and KMS key policy guidance) that w...
text: Summary: This extends the S3 destination adapter to support SSE-KMS (SSE with customer-managed keys) and an assume-role credential flow for cross-account bucket delivery. It also adds operator-facing documentation with minimal IAM policy snippets (bucket policy, role trust policy, and KMS key policy guidance) that we can share with beta customers. Motivation: During early beta onboarding we hit two recurring enterprise requirements: (1) buckets enforced with "s3:x-amz-server-side-encryption" conditions (must be aws:kms) and (2) cross-account delivery where the customer does not want to place a static access key in Redwood; instead they provide an IAM Role ARN that Redwood assumes (with optional ExternalId). This PR implements both requirements while keeping least-privilege defaults and avoiding secret leakage in logs. Design/behavior: - New S3 destination fields (config schema + valid...

title: Add exporter metrics (lag/retries/throughput), basic tracing, and structured logs
preview: Summary: This PR adds first-pass Prometheus metrics + basic tracing + structured logging to the audit-log-exporter service. Metrics are aligned to Redwood observability naming/cardinality standards (per Allison/Logan feedback) and provide the signals needed for initial dashboards/alerts (lag, throughput, retries, ad...
text: Summary: This PR adds first-pass Prometheus metrics + basic tracing + structured logging to the audit-log-exporter service. Metrics are aligned to Redwood observability naming/cardinality standards (per Allison/Logan feedback) and provide the signals needed for initial dashboards/alerts (lag, throughput, retries, adapter errors). This work is scoped to exporter-side instrumentation only; the control plane status endpoint will consume these signals via existing health paths and the observability pack dashboards will be done separately. Context: ENG-5168. Motivation: During beta soak we’ve been debugging exporter lag spikes and downstream 429/backpressure mostly via ad-hoc logs. We need stable metrics to (a) quantify export lag vs SLO, (b) separate downstream throttling from internal queueing, (c) support paging policy (page on sustained lag / hard failures, ticket on transient retries)...

title: Include authz decision context in SIEM export (ECS/CIM mapping)
preview:
Summary
Adds support for exporting RBAC v2 authorization decision context fields (subject/action/resource/scope/policy_version/decision/denial reasons) through the audit-log-exporter and maps them to common SIEM schema conventions.

This PR is intended to unblock enterprise security questionnaire asks and pilot cust...
text:
Summary
Adds support for exporting RBAC v2 authorization decision context fields (subject/action/resource/scope/policy_version/decision/denial reasons) through the audit-log-exporter and maps them to common SIEM schema conventions.

This PR is intended to unblock enterprise security questionnaire asks and pilot customer requirements where SIEM rules need to distinguish auth failures (deny) from application errors, and correlate access decisions with specific permission strings.

Context
- Redwood platform services are adding authz decision fields to audit log events (see redwood monorepo PR: redwood#28292).
- RBAC v2 requires evidence-friendly audit trails (SOC2 + enterprise questionnaires).
- Customers commonly ingest our audit logs into Splunk (CIM) and/or Elastic (ECS). They want stable field names, consistent typing, and predictable nullability.

What changed
1) New exported field...

title: Security: destination validation + egress allowlist hooks (SSRF/exfil mitigation)
preview: Motivation: This PR adds a defense-in-depth validation layer in the exporter to reduce SSRF and data-exfiltration risk by (a) validating destination endpoints for Splunk HEC and Datadog prior to use and (b) adding an egress allowlist hook that can be enforced by the runtime environment. We already validate some dest...
text: Motivation: This PR adds a defense-in-depth validation layer in the exporter to reduce SSRF and data-exfiltration risk by (a) validating destination endpoints for Splunk HEC and Datadog prior to use and (b) adding an egress allowlist hook that can be enforced by the runtime environment. We already validate some destination fields in the control plane, but we want the exporter to be safe even if a bad config slips through (migration bugs, stale cached config, or future destinations). Summary of changes: 1) Strict URL parsing + normalization: Introduce a shared DestinationURL parser that rejects ambiguous/invalid URLs, strips userinfo, normalizes scheme/host/port, and prevents path tricks (e.g., embedded @, backslashes, encoded host separators). 2) Blocklist for dangerous targets: Reject localhost, link-local, and RFC1918 / private ranges (including 127.0.0.0/8, ::1, 10.0.0.0/8, 172.16....

title: Support attaching retention config export to audit log evidence package
preview:
Context
Customers (and GRC internally) frequently request both (a) audit logs for a specified window and (b) an export of the tenant’s retention configuration (incl. defaults + overrides + change history pointer) as a single evidence package. This PR adds a first-class way to bundle the retention config export along...
text:
Context
Customers (and GRC internally) frequently request both (a) audit logs for a specified window and (b) an export of the tenant’s retention configuration (incl. defaults + overrides + change history pointer) as a single evidence package. This PR adds a first-class way to bundle the retention config export alongside the audit log export output.

Scope
- Add optional attachment support to evidence packages produced by audit-log-exporter.
- Implement a RetentionConfigAttachmentProvider which fetches retention config export from the control plane export endpoint and embeds it in the package.
- Update report template runner to allow "attachments" definitions (initially: retention_config).
- Add guardrails: size limits, explicit allowlist, and redaction check for known sensitive fields.
- Update CLI + HTTP API to accept the attachment flag(s).

Design notes
- Attachments are stored wit...

title: Add time-windowed exports + actor/event-type filters (report templates)
preview:
## Summary
This PR enhances `audit-log-exporter` to support compliance/evidence workflows by adding (1) explicit time-windowed exports and (2) optional filters for actor + event types. It also introduces a small report-template layer so the compliance reporting pack can request a known preset ("last_90_days_admin_ac...
text:
## Summary
This PR enhances `audit-log-exporter` to support compliance/evidence workflows by adding (1) explicit time-windowed exports and (2) optional filters for actor + event types. It also introduces a small report-template layer so the compliance reporting pack can request a known preset ("last_90_days_admin_activity", "rbac_changes", etc.) without re-encoding filter logic in multiple places.

Motivation: enterprise security reviews are consistently asking for "audit logs for a defined period" and "only admin/security-relevant events". We also had a support report (SUP-3917) where customers believed exports were missing event types; part of that was unclear defaults and lack of an explicit event-type filter list in the export request.

## API changes
### New request fields (non-breaking)
- `start_time` (RFC3339, inclusive)
- `end_time` (RFC3339, exclusive)
- `actor_ids` (optional...

title: Backpressure controls + max lag safety (auto-pause) for downstream outages
preview: Context: We had a beta incident where Splunk HEC throttling (429 + intermittent 503) caused exporter lag to spike and sustained retries increased queue pressure and CPU, impacting other tenants sharing the same exporter shard (see #incidents thread 1739559010 and SUP-18531 follow-up). This PR introduces explicit bac...
text:
Context: We had a beta incident where Splunk HEC throttling (429 + intermittent 503) caused exporter lag to spike and sustained retries increased queue pressure and CPU, impacting other tenants sharing the same exporter shard (see #incidents thread 1739559010 and SUP-18531 follow-up). This PR introduces explicit backpressure controls and a max-lag safety mechanism to keep the system stable during downstream outages while preserving at-least-once semantics.

What’s in this PR: (1) Max lag threshold per destination with auto-pause: if export_lag_seconds for a (tenant, destination) exceeds configurable max_lag_seconds for a sustained window, we stop pulling new batches for that destination and mark it PAUSED (reason=MAX_LAG) while continuing to process other destinations/tenants. (2) Backpressure based on in-flight bytes and retry backlog: introduce per-destination in_flight_bytes limits...

title: Bootstrap audit exporter service skeleton + CI + local dev harness
preview:
## Summary
Bootstraps the new `audit-log-exporter` service repository with a minimal runnable skeleton, CI workflows, a base configuration model, and a local dev harness to support early iteration.

This is the first landing step for ENG-5139. No adapters, no delivery semantics, and no production wiring in this PR; ...
text:
## Summary
Bootstraps the new `audit-log-exporter` service repository with a minimal runnable skeleton, CI workflows, a base configuration model, and a local dev harness to support early iteration.

This is the first landing step for ENG-5139. No adapters, no delivery semantics, and no production wiring in this PR; those will follow in subsequent PRs.

## Motivation / Context
We need a dedicated service to read audit events (initially via stubbed source in local dev), apply tenant scoping, and deliver to downstream destinations with retries/checkpointing. Before we can iterate on architecture and adapters, we need:
- Standard project layout + build tooling
- Baseline CI (lint/test/build, container build)
- A config model (typed) that can evolve with control-plane config sync
- A local harness so devs can run the service end-to-end without needing the full control plane

## What’s incl...

title: Add config sync from control plane (polling + streaming) with tenant scoping + caching
preview: Implements config sync from the control plane for the audit exporter service. This adds (1) tenant scoping so a given exporter instance only processes configs it is authorized to see, (2) a resilient polling loop with jitter/backoff and a long-poll “watch” mode when supported by the control plane, and (3) an in-memo...
text:
Implements config sync from the control plane for the audit exporter service. This adds (1) tenant scoping so a given exporter instance only processes configs it is authorized to see, (2) a resilient polling loop with jitter/backoff and a long-poll “watch” mode when supported by the control plane, and (3) an in-memory snapshot cache with safe defaults so we do not accidentally start exporting for a tenant unless the config is explicitly enabled.

Motivation
- ENG-5139 created the exporter scaffold; we need an end-to-end way to pull export destinations/filters from the control plane without requiring restarts.
- ENG-5132 is landing CRUD + validation in the control plane; this PR wires up the consumer side.

Key behaviors
- Startup: exporter boots in “no tenants enabled” state until it successfully fetches a config snapshot.
- Polling: periodic snapshot fetch (default 10s with +/-20% ji...

title: Add Datadog Logs destination adapter + audit tag/facet mapping
preview: Summary: This PR adds a Datadog Logs destination adapter to the audit-log-exporter and establishes a stable mapping from audit event schema v2 -> Datadog tags/facets. It also introduces rate-limit aware batching behavior (primarily for 429/503) so we degrade gracefully under Datadog intake constraints while preservi...
text: Summary: This PR adds a Datadog Logs destination adapter to the audit-log-exporter and establishes a stable mapping from audit event schema v2 -> Datadog tags/facets. It also introduces rate-limit aware batching behavior (primarily for 429/503) so we degrade gracefully under Datadog intake constraints while preserving at-least-once semantics. Context: As part of the SIEM-ready audit export beta, we support Splunk HEC, S3, and Datadog Logs. Datadog differs from Splunk in that (a) intake is strongly rate-limited, and (b) the best user experience is via consistent tagging/faceting for filtering/dashboards in Datadog. This PR aligns with ENG-5156 and the delivery semantics ADR (at-least-once, downstream dedup via event_id). Design notes (high-level): Adapter: Adds `datadog_logs` destination type with `site`, `intake_path`, and `api_key_secret_ref`. We send batches to `https://http-intake....

title: Filtering engine v1: allowlist/denylist by category/action/resource
preview: Implements filtering engine v1 for SIEM-ready audit export (ENG-5160). This adds a small, explicit rule model (allowlist/denylist) that can be evaluated per-event in the exporter with deterministic semantics and safe defaults. Scope in this PR: (1) filter model + evaluation logic (categories/actions/resource predica...
text: Implements filtering engine v1 for SIEM-ready audit export (ENG-5160). This adds a small, explicit rule model (allowlist/denylist) that can be evaluated per-event in the exporter with deterministic semantics and safe defaults. Scope in this PR: (1) filter model + evaluation logic (categories/actions/resource predicates), (2) strict config validation + normalization (including “deny wins” and invalid rule rejection), and (3) comprehensive unit tests for tricky cases (empty filters, conflicting allow/deny, unknown categories, wildcard handling, resource/action normalization, timestamp boundary behavior). Motivation: we need customer-configurable filtering that is auditable, easy to reason about during incident response, and stable enough to map to UI + control-plane API without frequent behavior changes. Semantics (as implemented): The effective policy is evaluated in this order: valida...

title: Add Splunk HEC destination adapter (gzip compression, 429 backoff, per-token circuit breaker)
preview: Summary: Implements a new Splunk HEC destination adapter for SIEM-ready audit exports. The adapter supports gzip-compressed payloads, robust handling of downstream throttling (429) with Retry-After-aware exponential backoff + jitter, and a per-token circuit breaker to avoid hot-looping on bad credentials or persiste...
text: Summary: Implements a new Splunk HEC destination adapter for SIEM-ready audit exports. The adapter supports gzip-compressed payloads, robust handling of downstream throttling (429) with Retry-After-aware exponential backoff + jitter, and a per-token circuit breaker to avoid hot-looping on bad credentials or persistent throttling. Motivation: Beta customers are primarily Splunk-first and we observed exporter lag spikes when HEC returns sustained 429s; this change improves delivery stability and reduces noisy retry behavior. Scope: Adapter implementation + config schema + basic unit tests + metrics hooks. Not included: Splunk indexer acknowledgment (HEC ACK) support (will track separately), advanced event field remapping beyond the audit schema’s SIEM mapping guidance. Delivery semantics: remains at-least-once; downstream Splunk consumers should dedupe on event_id if needed. Config: add...

title: Add dead-letter queue + guarded replay CLI for failed export batches
preview: Summary: This PR introduces a dead-letter queue (DLQ) for audit export batches that fail delivery after exhausting retries, and adds an operator-facing replay tool with guardrails. This is a reliability/safety feature for the SIEM-ready audit export beta (Splunk HEC / Datadog Logs / S3) to ensure we never silently d...
text: Summary: This PR introduces a dead-letter queue (DLQ) for audit export batches that fail delivery after exhausting retries, and adds an operator-facing replay tool with guardrails. This is a reliability/safety feature for the SIEM-ready audit export beta (Splunk HEC / Datadog Logs / S3) to ensure we never silently drop events while still protecting the platform and customers from accidental replay storms. Context / Motivation: During downstream outages (e.g., sustained 429s from Splunk HEC or 403s from mis-scoped Datadog keys), the exporter currently retries with backoff and then surfaces a per-destination error. Without DLQ persistence we risk either (a) losing batches on process/queue churn or (b) having no safe operational path to replay specific failures once a customer fixes credentials/quotas. ENG-5164 calls for a DLQ + replay tooling with explicit operator controls. Design high...

title: Support backup-oriented batch export mode (pagination + retention guardrails)
preview: Context: Private DR / compliance asks require a repeatable, non-interactive way to export audit logs for backup/retention evidence. The existing exporter is optimized for ad-hoc, interactive exports and can be slow/fragile when used in scheduled jobs (offset-based pagination, no checkpointing, limited safeguards aro...
text:
Context: Private DR / compliance asks require a repeatable, non-interactive way to export audit logs for backup/retention evidence. The existing exporter is optimized for ad-hoc, interactive exports and can be slow/fragile when used in scheduled jobs (offset-based pagination, no checkpointing, limited safeguards around retention boundaries).

This PR adds a backup-oriented batch export mode designed for scheduled backups:
- New CLI subcommand: `audit-log-exporter export batch`
- Cursor-based pagination using the API’s stable `after_id`/`next_cursor` semantics (no offset)
- Checkpointing/resume: writes a checkpoint token to disk periodically; supports `--resume` to continue without duplicating output
- Retention guardrails: validates requested time window against server-reported retention (if available) and fails fast with actionable messaging; explicit override flag required to procee...

title: Implement S3 destination adapter (batching, partitioning, checkpoints, retries)
preview:
Context: This PR adds the first destination adapter for the SIEM-ready audit export beta: S3 delivery. The adapter is built around (a) deterministic partitioning for cost/operability, (b) bounded batching for throughput, and (c) at-least-once delivery via checkpoint persistence.

Linear: ENG-5144

What’s included:
-...
text:
Context: This PR adds the first destination adapter for the SIEM-ready audit export beta: S3 delivery. The adapter is built around (a) deterministic partitioning for cost/operability, (b) bounded batching for throughput, and (c) at-least-once delivery via checkpoint persistence.

Linear: ENG-5144

What’s included:
- New S3 destination adapter implementing the exporter Destination interface
- Batching policy: flush on max_events, max_bytes, or flush_interval (defaults tuned for beta)
- Partitioning scheme (configurable prefix + time partitioning + tenant scoping) to avoid hot prefixes and keep search-friendly layouts
- Checkpoint persistence for at-least-once semantics (write object -> persist checkpoint). Cursor is (emitted_at, event_id) and is treated as an opaque ordering token by downstream
- Retry/backoff for S3 throttling/transient errors (SlowDown/503/timeouts) with jitter and a...
